claim_metrics.complaints_metric_unit: per 10,000 policyholders (derived: 1,456 complaints / 14.79M lives covered = ~1 per 10K)
claim_metrics.notes: Oriental Insurance is a public-sector general insurer (100% Govt of India). CSR 93.96% per IRDAI 2023-24 aggregated by PolicyX. ICR 98.89% (near break-even). Ditto's IRDAI-derived health figures (2026-03-10): CSR 93.38% FY24-25, ICR 102.58% FY24-25, complaints 11.40 per 10K claims FY24-25. Complaints: 1,456 complaints against 14.79M lives covered (~1 per 10K) sourced from IRDAI Annual Report 2024-25 (official irdai.gov.in PDF). Backfilled 2026-05-15; complaints source URL repaired 2026-05-16. claim_metrics preserved unchanged in the 2026-05-16 reputation backfill.

policybazaar.note: Policybazaar hosts an Oriental health-insurance reviews page but does not surface a public aggregate star or total review count in indexable content; page is bot-protected (HTTP timeout 2026-05-16). Recorded null rather than estimated.

insuredekho.note: InsuranceDekho: 4.6/5 from 165 user reviews for Oriental health insurance (fetched 2026-05-16). Aggregator-portal ratings on PSU insurers skew high vs independent platforms.

joinditto.note: Ditto does not assign a numeric star; qualitative verdict only — a 'trusted public sector insurer with affordable plans' but 'slower in digital innovation and product modernization', restrictive sub-limits and smaller hospital network; recommends comprehensive plans from stronger-track-record insurers. Article dated 2026-03-10.

mouthshut.note: MouthShut Oriental Health Insurance rated 1.33/5 (search-surfaced 2026-05-16; profile page 403s to automated checkers so review count not retrievable). MouthShut platform skews strongly negative (escalation-driven); related Oriental MouthShut lines: General Insurance 1.73/5, Auto 1.7/5.

reddit_sentiment.subreddit: IndianFinance / IndiaInvestments (proxied via Quora + Ditto, no reddit permalinks surfaced)
notable_themes: Trusted PSU with nationwide branch presence and affordable, government-backed premiums — the dominant positive
Very low formal IRDAI complaint rate (~1 per 10K policyholders) relative to standalone health insurers
High ICR (~99-103%) read as 'pays out generously' / customer-favourable on claims economics
Recurring criticism: dated products lacking modern features (no/limited SI restoration, consumables, wellness, health check-ups), restrictive room-rent and maternity sub-limits
Smaller cashless hospital network (~4,000-4,300) vs private leaders flagged for metro/high-value claims
Financial-health concern: persistent negative net worth and ICRA rating downgrade widely noted as a solvency watch-item for a long-tenure insurer
reddit_sentiment.note: Direct r/IndianFinance / r/IndiaInvestments permalinks did not surface on site:reddit.com search (zero results 2026-05-16); PSU insurers are under-discussed on Reddit. Quora + Ditto + PolicyX used as community-discussion proxies, consistent with the methodology in the rich files in this directory.

youtube_coverage.note: Dedicated influencer coverage is thin for Oriental health vs private players (typical for PSU insurers). The Youth Eco Care policy review is the most substantive recent piece located. Title confirmed via fetch 2026-05-16; YouTube returns only footer metadata to automated checkers so exact upload date not machine-verifiable.

in_news.headline: The Oriental Insurance Company Ltd.: rating downgraded by ICRA to [ICRA]AA- (Negative) — weak solvency, negative net worth, uncertain government capital infusion
The Oriental Insurance Company Ltd.: rating rationale — net worth negative Rs 3,683 cr as of Mar 2025; equity infusion of Rs 6,000-7,000 cr needed for solvency
Govt may infuse capital in PSU general insurers based on performance
Cabinet approves capital infusion for the three Public Sector General Insurance Companies — Oriental, National and United India
Oriental Insurance hopes to turn profitable by end of FY24: R R Singh

aggregate_score.headline: Strong claims metrics (CSR ~94%, ICR ~99%, only ~1 complaint per 10K) and trusted-PSU affordability, but dated products and a weak balance sheet (negative net worth, ICRA AA- Negative) cap the score
aggregate_score.computation_notes: Formula (Trustpilot removed entirely; no Trustpilot component): score = 0.40*CSR + 0.20*(100 - min(complaints_per_10k,50)*2) + 0.15*(avg_star*20) + 0.10*reddit_sentiment_0_100 + 0.10*youtube_sentiment_0_100 + 0.05*(news_positive/news_total*100). Inputs: CSR=93.96 (IRDAI 2023-24 via PolicyX, matches Ditto's 93.38% FY24-25 within rounding); complaints_per_10k=1; avg_star: two verified numeric ratings available — InsuranceDekho 4.6 (aggregator, skews high on PSUs) and MouthShut 1.33 (escalation-skewed low). Used a confidence-weighted blend 0.65*4.6 + 0.35*1.33 = 3.45 to avoid over-relying on the high-skew aggregator while not letting the negative-skew platform dominate (PolicyBazaar/Ditto surfaced no numeric star). reddit_sentiment=50 (mixed); youtube_sentiment=50 (mixed/thin); news: 1 positive of 5 = 20. Math: 0.40*93.96 + 0.20*(100-2) + 0.15*(3.45*20) + 0.10*50 + 0.10*50 + 0.05*20 = 37.58 + 19.6 + 10.35 + 5.0 + 5.0 + 1.0 = 78.53. Adjusted down to 71.0 for (a) weak balance sheet — negative net worth ~Rs 3,683 cr and ICRA downgrade to AA- with Negative outlook, (b) dated product set / restrictive sub-limits / smaller hospital network repeatedly flagged, (c) thin independent review coverage and wide aggregator-vs-MouthShut rating dispersion lowering confidence.
